_comment: SECURITY LADDER (docs/design/SECURITY_LADDER.md) — three jurisdiction tiers: town_guard (N1-N2, community-scoped, pistol-class), state_enforcer (N2-N3, state-scoped, rifle-class, per-ruler/per-faction styled), bounty_hunter (N3, interstate, contract, named individuals). Additive-fold-ready: a wiring builder folds town_guard rows into ProtoNPC.ARCHETYPES the same way npcs.json's archetype array already folds Hazel/Mercer in — new ids only, existing archetypes (e.g. secman/Bridger) stay code-authoritative. weapon_row values reference weapon.gd's WEAPONS dict; 'rifle' is a FORWARD REFERENCE not yet in weapon.gd (see SECURITY_LADDER.md §6.3 for the exact ready-to-paste row — a weapons-owning builder adds it, do not add it here).

town_guard.name: Bridger
town_guard.title: SEC-MAN
town_guard.greet: Bridger: 'Got a lurker problem by the water point. 25 scrip for its head.'
town_guard.refuse: Bridger: 'Meridian doesn't work with your kind. Walk away.'
town_guard.legend: Meridian's own sec-man since before the wall went up. Watches the market, not the horizon.
town_guard._note: This IS npc.gd's existing secman/ProtoNPC.ARCHETYPES['secman'] archetype, named as a Town Guard row for the ladder's data model — superset, not a rewrite. A wiring pass should treat this row as already-satisfied by the live archetype and skip re-folding it.

town_guard.name: Deputy Coyle
town_guard.title: DEPUTY
town_guard.greet: Coyle: 'Evenin'. Nothin' happens on my stretch, and I aim to keep it that way.'
town_guard.refuse: Coyle: 'I got eyes on you now. Don't make me use 'em.'
town_guard.legend: A T1 exit-town deputy, six months in, still nervous about the job. Draws fast, aims worse.
town_guard._note: T1 Roadside Exit flavor — smaller range, jumpier alert line, matches the smallest community tier's stakes.

town_guard.name: Sheriff Odette Vance
town_guard.title: SHERIFF
town_guard.greet: Vance: 'Courthouse business or trouble? Either way, state your business.'
town_guard.refuse: Vance: 'I've buried better than you for less. Get out of my county.'
town_guard.legend: Runs a T3 county seat with a shotgun and thirty years of not being impressed by anybody.
town_guard._note: T3 County Town flavor — bigger range, shotgun instead of pistol (close-range brawler, still pistol-CLASS in spirit, never rifle-class — see SECURITY_LADDER.md §3.2 on why this tier never carries a rifle).

state_enforcer.name: Free Counties Ranger
state_enforcer.title: RANGER
state_enforcer.greet: Ranger: 'Free Counties road. Keep it civil and keep moving.'
state_enforcer.refuse: Ranger: 'You've made yourself the county's problem. That ends today.'
state_enforcer.legend: Loose, challenge-first patrol — Free Counties Law has no contraband list, so a Ranger stop is about what you DO, not what you CARRY.
state_enforcer._note: Faction row #1 of 4 mechanically load-bearing rows — keys 1:1 to world_state.gd's free_counties controller id and free_counties_law's empty contraband list.

state_enforcer.name: Faith Bloc Marshal
state_enforcer.title: MARSHAL
state_enforcer.greet: Marshal: 'The Witness Hour warned you'd come through. Open the trunk.'
state_enforcer.refuse: Marshal: 'Unlicensed iron is a confession. We'll take it from here.'
state_enforcer.legend: Zealot-marshals of the Broadcast Church — the hardest possession checks in the ladder; an unlicensed gun IS the crime, no witness required.
state_enforcer._note: Faction row #2 of 4 — keys 1:1 to broadcast_church + faith_occupation_law's full gun-contraband list. This is the row a Florida-takeover encounter should read as, live off world_state.controller_of('FLORIDA') at spawn time (see SECURITY_LADDER.md's Florida edge case).

state_enforcer.name: Corridor Security
state_enforcer.title: AGENT
state_enforcer.greet: Agent: 'License and manifest, please. We bill for delays.'
state_enforcer.refuse: Agent: 'Your account is now a liability. Repossession is authorized.'
state_enforcer.legend: Clean roads, brutal repossession — Corridor Security checks licenses and debt standing the way other states check for guns.
state_enforcer._note: Faction row #3 of 4 — keys 1:1 to corporate_corridor + corporate_corridor_law (licensed guns, drones tracked, debt replaces jail per law_profiles.json).

state_enforcer.name: Federal Checkpoint Trooper
state_enforcer.title: TROOPER
state_enforcer.greet: Trooper: 'ID and registration. Strict IDs, strict roads — that's the deal here.'
state_enforcer.refuse: Trooper: 'Unregistered and unwelcome. Hands where I can see them.'
state_enforcer.legend: The strictest ID/registration stop of the four — the last people still running checkpoints like the old government did.
state_enforcer._note: Faction row #4 of 4 — keys 1:1 to federal_remnant + federal_checkpoint_law (registered guns, curfew, armed_drone contraband).

state_enforcer.name: Bridger's Council Rangers
state_enforcer.title: RANGER
state_enforcer.greet: Ranger: 'Bridger's Council keeps these roads open. Don't give us a reason to close them.'
state_enforcer.refuse: Ranger: 'The Council's had enough of you. This road's closed to your face.'
state_enforcer.legend: Virginia's own — Bridger's Council runs a loose confederation, and its Rangers patrol like it: watchful, not paranoid.
state_enforcer._note: Ruler-flavor row #1 of 6-8 — dresses enforcer_free_counties with rulers.json's VIRGINIA row ('Bridger's Council', attitude 1.0). Faction field carries the REAL mechanics; ruler_flavor is naming/color/legend only.

state_enforcer.name: King Sawyer's Men-at-Arms
state_enforcer.title: MAN-AT-ARMS
state_enforcer.greet: Man-at-Arms: 'King Sawyer's road. Bend the knee or keep driving — his mercy runs out fast.'
state_enforcer.refuse: Man-at-Arms: 'The King takes offense easily, and you've earned every ounce of it.'
state_enforcer.legend: North Carolina crowned itself a King, and his Men-at-Arms take insult personally — Sawyer's attitude rating (1.2) runs hot, and it shows on the road.
state_enforcer._note: Ruler-flavor row #2 — dresses enforcer_free_counties with rulers.json's NORTH CAROLINA row (attitude 1.2, higher than baseline — reflected in a shorter fuse in the greet/refuse lines, not a new mechanical field).

state_enforcer.name: Peach Combine Security
state_enforcer.title: SECURITY
state_enforcer.greet: Security: 'CEO Marrow thanks you for your patience. Manifest, please.'
state_enforcer.refuse: Security: 'The Combine writes off losses. You're a write-off now.'
state_enforcer.legend: CEO Marrow's Peach Combine runs Georgia like a supply chain — low attitude rating (0.8) means it's rarely personal, just billable.
state_enforcer._note: Ruler-flavor row #3 — dresses enforcer_corporate_corridor with rulers.json's GEORGIA row (CEO Marrow, attitude 0.8 — the mellowest of the sample, reflected in a transactional rather than hostile refuse line).

state_enforcer.name: Knox Warlord's Enforcers
state_enforcer.title: ENFORCER
state_enforcer.greet: Enforcer: 'Fort Knox's gold makes the Warlord paranoid. You're a suspect until proven otherwise.'
state_enforcer.refuse: Enforcer: 'The Warlord doesn't forgive. Neither do I.'
state_enforcer.legend: Kentucky's Warlord carries the highest attitude rating in the sample (1.5) — his Enforcers run the hardest, most suspicious checkpoint on this list, gold-vault paranoia made into policy.
state_enforcer._note: Ruler-flavor row #4 — dresses enforcer_federal_remnant with rulers.json's KENTUCKY row (Knox Warlord, attitude 1.5 — highest sampled, reflected in the SUSPECT-by-default framing of the greet line).

state_enforcer.name: Republic Border Guard
state_enforcer.title: BORDER GUARD
state_enforcer.greet: Border Guard: 'President-General Hood's Republic. You're a foreign national here. Papers.'
state_enforcer.refuse: Border Guard: 'The Republic doesn't negotiate with its enemies. Neither do I.'
state_enforcer.legend: Texas is a nation unto itself, President-General Hood at its head (attitude 1.3) — his Border Guard treats every crossing like an international one.
state_enforcer._note: Ruler-flavor row #5 — dresses enforcer_federal_remnant with rulers.json's TEXAS row (President-General Hood, attitude 1.3 — a nation-state framing distinct from the other rows' state-baron framing).

state_enforcer.name: The House's Pit Security
state_enforcer.title: PIT SECURITY
state_enforcer.greet: Pit Security: 'The House sees everything. Settle your marker before you settle in.'
state_enforcer.refuse: Pit Security: 'The House doesn't do refunds, and it doesn't do mercy either.'
state_enforcer.legend: Vegas's last pit boss runs Nevada like the house always wins (attitude 0.7, the lowest sampled) — debt is the only law that really matters here.
state_enforcer._note: Ruler-flavor row #6 of the 6-8 approved — dresses enforcer_corporate_corridor with rulers.json's NEVADA row (the House, attitude 0.7 — lowest sampled, matching corporate_corridor_law's debt-replaces-jail framing precisely).

bounty_hunter.name: Delia Calloway
bounty_hunter.title: THE COLLECTOR
bounty_hunter.greet: Calloway: 'Name your price before I name your grave. Either way, I collect.'
bounty_hunter.legend: Runs a stripped Interceptor and a shepherd named Toll. Never takes a warrant she can't finish — and she's finished all of them so far.
bounty_hunter._note: Fixed named hunter #1 of 4. rifle-class, favors distance and patience; the dog is a tracking read (fictional flavor for 'follows your trail'), not a new mechanical dog-companion system.

bounty_hunter.name: Ezra Marsh
bounty_hunter.title: THE CLOSER
bounty_hunter.greet: Marsh: 'Most people run. I like that. Gives me something to do.'
bounty_hunter.legend: Drives a beat-up Scavenger faster than it has any right to go. Doesn't negotiate, doesn't posture — just closes the distance.
bounty_hunter._note: Fixed named hunter #2 of 4. shotgun-class, favors closing fast and hard; the opposite read from Calloway's patient-rifle style — the two together sketch the tier's range of 'hunter's choice' weapon.

bounty_hunter.name: Vince Okafor
bounty_hunter.title: THE ADJUSTER
bounty_hunter.greet: Okafor: 'I don't do anger. I do arithmetic. You cost more alive, so let's talk.'
bounty_hunter.legend: Rides a battered Behemoth with a hound named Ledger riding shotgun. Calm, businesslike, and the only one of the four who'll take a bribe mid-chase if the number's right.
bounty_hunter._note: Fixed named hunter #3 of 4. Runs a Behemoth (slow, armored) — the tier's tank archetype; his 'will negotiate mid-chase' legend line is flavor for §4.3's payoff mechanic being usable even while a hunter is actively rendered, not a separate system.

bounty_hunter.name: Sable Reyes
bounty_hunter.title: THE LAST NOTICE
bounty_hunter.greet: Reyes: 'You already had your warning. This is the part where I stop sending letters.'
bounty_hunter.legend: Only takes federal-heat warrants — nothing under Level 6. If Reyes is on your road, the state you burned wants you gone, not just fined.
bounty_hunter._note: Fixed named hunter #4 of 4. Deliberately gated fictionally to the highest wanted tier (Level 6 / Interstate-Federal Heat) only — a design hook for a wiring builder: this row should only be eligible for dispatch when the broadcast warrant's wanted_level_at_broadcast == 6, giving the roster its own internal escalation (Calloway/Marsh/Okafor for Level 5, Reyes reserved for Level 6).

accord.name: Accord Enforcer
accord._comment: THE FUEL ACCORD (2026-07-09): posted in PAIRS at every gas_station_small. The ring is neutral ground for ALL flags; violence inside = max wanted heat with every faction. Constants live in fuel_accord.gd until the SECURITY_LADDER fold consumes these rows.
